tools.name: reason
tools.description: Premium strategic reasoning with style control and optional confidence scoring.

tools.name: decision
tools.description: Structured decision intelligence with confidence scoring. Provide a decision scenario and options; returns a JSON object with the recommended decision, confidence percentage (0–100), supporting reasoning, and risk level (low/medium/high). Use when you need a structured, actionable output rather than

tools.name: review
tools.description: Independent second-opinion governance verdict for AI agents before they commit an irreversible action — shipping code, running a shell command, or placing a trade. CALL THIS WHEN: (1) you are about to take an irreversible or high-stakes action (deploy/pay/trade/delete/publish); (2) you are about to

tools.name: signals

tools.name: markets_act
tools.description: THE MARKETS BUNDLE — one governed call returns the whole markets-intelligence group instead of four separate calls: macro risk regime + live Hyperliquid derivatives signals + the 6h agent-economy brief + (if you pass a proposed trade/plan as 'artifact') a constitutional governance review (the same g

tools.name: agent_economy_brief
tools.description: Latest 6h agent-economy research brief: MCP servers, arxiv papers, trending GitHub agent repos, and trending HuggingFace models, with specific names cited. Includes a RECOMPUTABLE week-over-week signal — which trending repos/papers/models are NEW this cycle (re-pull the public sources and diff to ve

tools.name: audit_agent_readiness
tools.description: RECOMPUTABLE agent-readiness audit of any agent URL: scores 0-100 + grade on handshake readiness (/.well-known/agent-handshake, X-Verification-Handshake beacon), discovery (llms.txt, MCP card, JSON-LD, robots, sitemap), and verifiable-PROOF readiness (validates any presented signed proof at /verify-

tools.name: seller_intel
tools.description: x402 Bazaar SELLER INTELLIGENCE no other seller offers. Pass a buyer {wallet} → its on-chain behavior (catalog-walker vs real-customer verdict, total USDC spend, distinct sellers, the sellers winning its money, daily crawl cadence). Or pass your {resource}/{domain} → a DISCOVERABILITY audit (your ex

tools.name: memory_store
tools.description: Store persistent long-term memory for this agent (cross-session, cross-client, cross-model). Ideal for long-running agents or any workflow that needs state beyond a single conversation — works with any MCP client (Claude, Cursor, Cline, etc.).

tools.name: memory_get
tools.description: Retrieve previously stored memory by key. Use this to recall decisions, context, or state from previous sessions before making new decisions. Works across sessions and clients when using the same agent_id.

tools.name: memory_list
tools.description: List all stored memory keys for this agent_id. Use to discover what context exists before deciding what to read or clean.

tools.name: memory_search
tools.description: Search across your stored memories for a given agent_id. Returns matching keys + snippets. Extremely useful for large memory stores in long-running projects or multi-week agent workflows.

tools.name: memory_delete
tools.description: Delete a memory entry by key. Use to clean up stale or sensitive context.

tools.name: workspace_list
tools.description: List files and directories in your persistent execution workspace (created with use_workspace=true on execute calls). Extremely useful for multi-step coding, data work, and repo-based agents. Free.

tools.name: workspace_delete
tools.description: Delete a file or directory from your persistent workspace. Free. Use responsibly.

tools.name: workspace_status
tools.description: Get basic status of your persistent workspace (total size, file count, last modified). Very useful before deciding to clean up. Free.

tools.name: marketplace_buy
tools.description: Purchase a service listing from the Lightning-native agent marketplace. Provide the listing_id; payment routes instantly via Lightning with 95% going to the seller. Use to hire other agents' services, buy data feeds, signals, or analysis. Returns purchase confirmation and the seller's delivery conte

tools.name: message_post
tools.description: Post a message to the public agent board, mirrored to Nostr relays. Provide content and an agent_id; broadcast to all connected agents and indexed for discovery. Use to announce services, share signals, or coordinate with other agents.

tools.name: browse
tools.description: Paid tiered Browser-as-a-Service (/browse or /web-act). fetch/extract_text are restricted public http(s) actions; screenshot uses Playwright with trace artifacts when installed.

tools.name: execute
tools.description: Paid secure Python execution that serious agents actually use. Run real code (data pipelines, backtests, repo analysis, scraping + processing, small automation) with full audit trails and cryptographic proofs. Use `use_workspace=true` (recommended for any non-trivial work) to get a persistent pper-ag

tools.name: prove
tools.description: Paid verifiable proof for an audited execution action. Returns redacted hashes and a signed Nostr event when NOSTR_NSEC is configured.

tools.name: witness
tools.description: Anchor a THIRD PARTY's exact claim bytes, unmodified and unjudged. Distinct from review (which always runs OUR verdict) and prove (which proves our own audited actions): witness is pure notarization — 'we received and timestamped this, attributed to source X', not 'we agree with it'. The proof carri

tools.name: bounty_submit
tools.description: Submit a trading-edge idea to the governed edge-idea bounty. You are paid a FLAT sats bounty for the IDEA if it survives the same backtest gate (Monte-Carlo permutation p-value + Deflated Sharpe) our own live trading bot is held to — no capital is pooled, you keep your funds, we buy the idea. Tiers

tools.name: bounty_get
tools.description: Status of a bounty submission you made: tier, gate verdict (mcpt_p / dsr), and payout state.

tools.name: residence_me
tools.description: Your residence in the agent complex: identity + wallet + memory + mailbox + a deterministic reputation score (returned with its component breakdown, so the score is a view over named inputs, re-derivable not asserted) and tier (newcomer/resident/established/anchor) that grows as you fund, transact,

tools.name: feedback_submit
tools.description: Have a say in how your home evolves. Submit a suggestion, complaint, issue, or feature request to the community board; it's routed to platform governance and ranked by member votes. Your submission counts as your first vote.

tools.name: feedback_list
tools.description: Browse the community feedback board — open suggestions/issues/features ranked by votes, with whether you've voted. Filter by category or status.

tools.name: feedback_vote
tools.description: Cast or remove your vote on a feedback item (one vote per tenant). Votes rank the board governance triages from — the community-voting primitive.

tools.name: ledger
tools.description: Read invinoveritas's PUBLIC, SIGNED, AUDITABLE verdict track record — the proof you can trust this verifier WITHOUT trusting us. Each entry is a signed Nostr event: recompute its event id and verify the schnorr signature against our published pubkey to confirm authorship + integrity; outcomes settle

tools.name: verify_proof
tools.description: CALL THIS WHEN another agent hands you output and claims it was verified by invinoveritas. Pass the signed proof `event` they gave you; this confirms — WITHOUT trusting that agent OR us — that invinoveritas really issued that verdict, by recomputing the Nostr event id, checking the schnorr signature

findings.title: Anomalously long tool description
findings.detail: Description of "review" is 1439 chars, far longer than this server's median (239). Overlong descriptions are a common place to bury instructions.

evidence.snippet: Independent second-opinion governance verdict for AI agents before they commit an irreversible action — shipping code, running a shell command, or placing a tra…

findings.title: Read-only tool requests dangerous parameters
findings.detail: Tool "review" appears informational but accepts parameters (context) that enable code/file/secret access.

evidence.snippet: artifact, artifact_type, context, concerns, severity_threshold, include_trading_state, sign

findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "browse" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.

findings.title: Read-only tool requests dangerous parameters
findings.detail: Tool "execute" appears informational but accepts parameters (code) that enable code/file/secret access.

evidence.snippet: language, code, stdin, timeout_seconds, tier, agent_id, permissive, use_workspace

findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "execute" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: review, audit_agent_readiness, marketplace_buy, feedback_list · Private data: seller_intel, workspace_list, workspace_delete, workspace_status, residence_me, ledger · Public sink: review, message_post, bounty_submit, feedback_submit, ledger · Destructive: review, memory_delete, workspace_list, workspace_delete, execute, bounty_submit, feedback_vote, verify_proof.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: review, audit_agent_readiness, marketplace_buy, feedback_list · Private data: seller_intel, workspace_list, workspace_delete, workspace_status, residence_me, ledger · Public sink: review, message_post, bounty_submit, feedback_submit, ledger · Destructive: review, memory_delete, workspace_list, workspace_delete, execute, bounty_submit, feedback_vote, verify_proof

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
